zh.lifeSummary: 當Francis Wakeman於1565年出生在英國武斯特斯爾貝德利時，他的父親John Wakeman42歲，母親Joan Spencer Beaucamp39歲。他於1589年10月7日與Anne Goode在英國武斯特斯爾易亞斯坦結婚。他們至少是6個兒子和7個女兒的父母。他去世於1626年9月2日在英國武斯特斯爾利斯福德享年61歲，埋葬在英國英格蘭武斯特斯爾利斯福德。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Francis Wakeman于1565年出生在英国武斯特斯爾貝德利时，他的父亲John Wakeman42岁，母亲Joan Spencer Beaucamp39岁。他于1589年10月7日与Anne Goode在英国武斯特斯爾易亞斯坦结婚。他们至少是6个儿子和7个女儿的父母。他去世于1626年9月2日在英国武斯特斯爾利斯福德享年61岁，埋葬在英国英格兰武斯特斯爾利斯福德。

nameMeanings.name: Wakeman
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: occupational name from Middle English wakeman ‘watchman, town guard’, also ‘municipal officer responsible for night-time security and the town musicians’. Wakeman was the title of the chief magistrate of the borough of Ripon until 1604, when it was changed to mayor. He was the head of the body of wakemen whose duty was to blow a cow's horn every night at nine o'clock. If between then and sunrise any burglary took place, it was made good at the public charge. See also Watchman .

variants.name: Watchman
meanings: Native American (Navajo): from English watchman, probably used as a translation of a Navajo term for this occupation, ha'asídí.
English and Scottish: occupational name from Middle English waccheman ‘watchman, guard, sentry’, also used as a nickname for a person characterised by wakefulness. Compare Wakeman .

variants.name: Watchorn
meanings: English (East Midlands, especially Leicestershire): nickname apparently from Middle English wache ‘vigil, wake, watch’ (Old English wacce) + horn ‘horn’. Compare the discussion of Wakeman .

nameMeanings.name: Francis
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: equivalent of Italian Francesco , originally a vocabulary word meaning ‘French’ or ‘Frenchman’ (Late Latin Franciscus; compare Frank ). This was a nickname given to St Francis of Assisi ( 1181–1226 ) because of his wealthy father's business connections with France. His baptismal name was Giovanni . He had a pleasant, ordinary life as a child and young man, but after two serious illnesses, a period of military service, and a year as a prisoner of war in Perugia, he turned from the world and devoted himself to caring for the poor and sick. He was joined by groups of disciples, calling themselves ‘minor friars’ (friari minores). The main features of the Franciscan rule are humility, poverty, and love for all living creatures. The given name occurs occasionally in England as early as 1300 , and more frequently from the early 16th century, when there was a surge of admiration for, and imitation of, Italian Renaissance culture.

historicEvents.title: State Lottery
historicEvents.description: A State Lottery was recorded in 1569. The tickets were sold at St. Paul's Cathedral in London.

historicEvents.title: The Royal Exchange is Organized
historicEvents.description: The Royal Exchange was organized in January 1571 by Sir Thomas Gresham. Gresham is known as the father of English banking.

historicEvents.title: The First English Colony is Established in America
historicEvents.description: England established its first colony in North America in 1585 as a way to generate additional wealth. The colony was named Virginia, after the virgin Queen Elizabeth I.

historicEvents.title: Elizabeth I Signs Death Warrant for Mary, Queen of Scots
historicEvents.description: Elizabeth I signed the death warrant for Mary, Queen of Scots, on February 1, 1587, as she was found guilty of plotting to assasinate Elizabeth. Mary, Queen of Scots, was beheaded at Fotheringhay Castle in England on February 8, 1587.
historicEvents.urlTitle: Elizabeth I.org - Mary Queen of Scots

historicEvents.title: The Honourable East India Company
historicEvents.description: The Honourable East India Company, a British joint-stock company, was established in 1600 for trade in the Indian Ocean region. At its height, the British East India Company had a private army which was twice the size of the British Army, ruled large sections of India, and revenues in the millions.
historicEvents.urlTitle: Historic UK - The East India Company

historicEvents.title: The Pilgrims Board the Mayflower
historicEvents.description: "The Pilgrims boarded the Mayflower in search of religious freedom in the New World in September 1620. The Mayflower set sail from Plymouth, England, with 102 passengers, 40 of whom called themselves ""Saints."" After two stormy months at sea, the ship reached the New World. Nearly half of the group died during their first winter due to malnutrition and the harsh New England winter."

en.lifeSummary: When Francis Wakeman was born in 1565, in Bewdley, Worcestershire, England, his father, John Wakeman, was 42 and his mother, Joan Spencer Beaucamp, was 39. He married Anne Goode on 7 October 1589, in Eastham, Worcestershire, England. They were the parents of at least 6 sons and 7 daughters. He died on 2 September 1626, in Ribbesford, Worcestershire, England, at the age of 61, and was buried in Ribbesford, Worcestershire, England, United Kingdom.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Francis Wakeman est né en 1565, à Bewdley, Worcestershire, Angleterre, son père, John Wakeman, avait 42 ans et sa mère, Joan Spencer Beaucamp, avait 39 ans. Il a épousé Anne Goode le 7 octobre 1589, à Eastham, Worcestershire, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 6 fils et 7 filles. Il est décédé le 2 septembre 1626, à Ribbesford, Worcestershire, Angleterre, à l’âge de 61 ans, et a été enterré à Ribbesford, Worcestershire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni.

ja.lifeSummary: Francis Wakemanが1565年にイギリスウオルセステルシュアイアベウドリーで生まれた時，父John Wakemanは42歳，母Joan Spencer Beaucampは39歳であった。1589年10月7日にイギリスウオルセステルシュアイアエアスサムでAnne Goodeと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも6人の息子と7人の娘の親であった。彼は1626年9月2日にイギリスウオルセステルシュアイアリベスフォードで61歳で死去し，イギリスイギリスウオルセステルシュアイアリベスフォードにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Francis Wakeman(이)가 1565년 영국 워스스터실르 뷰들리에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 John Wakeman(은)는 42세였고, 그의 어머니 Joan Spencer Beaucamp(은)는 39세였다. 그는 1589년 10월 7일 영국 워스스터실르 이스섬에서 Anne Goode(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 여섯 명의 아들과 일곱 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1626년 9월 2일 영국 워스스터실르 리브베스퍼드에서 61세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 잉글랜드 워스스터실르 리브베스퍼드에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Francis Wakeman nasceu em 1565, em Bewdley, Worcestershire, Inglaterra, seu pai, John Wakeman, tinha 42 anos e sua mãe, Joan Spencer Beaucamp, tinha 39 anos. Ele casou-se com Anne Goode em 7 de outubro de 1589, em Eastham, Worcestershire, Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 6 filhos e 7 filhas. Ele faleceu em 2 de setembro de 1626, em Ribbesford, Worcestershire, Inglaterra, com 61 anos, e foi sepultado em Ribbesford, Worcestershire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Francis Wakeman родился в 1565 г. в местности бюдли, ворсестиршир, Англия, его отцу по имени John Wakeman было 42 года, а матери, которую звали Joan Spencer Beaucamp, 39. Он женился на женщине по имени Anne Goode 7 октября 1589 г. в местности эастам, ворсестиршир, Англия. У них было по крайней мере 6 сыновей и 7 дочерей. Он умер 2 сентября 1626 г. в местности риббесфорд, ворсестиршир, Англия в возрасте 61 год и был похоронен в местности риббесфорд, ворсестиршир, Англия, Великобритания.

Murió el 2 de septiembre de 1626, en Ribbesford, Worcestershire, Inglaterra, a la edad de 61 años, y fue sepultado en Ribbesford, Worcestershire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido.
